A three-chapter amateur-detective mystery set in a Pacific-Northwest
oyster-farming co-op. POV: Margo, the co-op's bookkeeper, who notices
the week's tide-chart has been edited after the fact and realises
someone is using the off-hours oyster rafts to move something heavier
than shellfish. A body turns up in the spat-catching nets.

Three chapters: (1) the tide-chart discrepancy, (2) Margo reconstructs
the night's movements from receipts and fuel logs, (3) the co-op
meeting where Margo lays out the case. Fair-play: every clue the
reader needs appears in the text; red herrings are real misreads, not
withheld information.
